zh.lifeSummary: 當George English Dalton於1535年出生在英國素福爾可易普斯維其時，他的父親William Dalton22歲，母親Jane Towneley20歲。他於1550年與Ruth Parkhurst在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1614年2月24日在英國英格蘭素福爾可丹寧頓享年79歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可丹寧頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当George English Dalton于1535年出生在英国素福爾可易普斯維其时，他的父亲William Dalton22岁，母亲Jane Towneley20岁。他于1550年与Ruth Parkhurst在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1614年2月24日在英国英格兰素福爾可丹寧頓享年79岁，埋葬在英国英格兰素福爾可丹寧頓。

nameMeanings.name: Dalton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Dalton in Westmorland, Durham, Lancashire, Northumberland, and Yorkshire, from Old English dæl ‘valley’ (see Dale ) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.
History: The surname was taken to Ireland by English traders in the early 13th century .

variants.name: Daughton
meanings: English (Lincolnshire) and Irish (Kilkenny and Tipperary): variant of   Dalton  .

variants.name: Dale
meanings: English: from Middle English dal, dale, daile ‘dale, valley’ (Old English dæl, reinforced in northern England by the cognate Old Norse dalr), a topographic name for someone who lived in a valley, or a habitational name from any of numerous minor places called with this word, such as Dale in Cumbria and Yorkshire.
Norwegian: habitational name from a common farm named from Old Norse dali, the dative case of dalr ‘valley’.

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: English
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Welsh: ethnic name from Middle English English, Inglish, Inglis ‘English’ (Old English Englisc), sometimes alternating with Anglo-Norman French Engleis, Engles, Anglais, Angles (Old French Englois). Compare Inglis and England . Among the aristocracy and upper gentry it marked out a man of English ancestry from one of Norman or continental origin. In counties bordering England with Scotland and Wales the name distinguished an Englishman from a Scot or a native Welshman on both sides of the border. The name may also have been acquired by English merchants who traded abroad or who lived and worked in a ‘French’ borough in England (one exclusively administered by Normans).
Irish: in Ireland, this name was used to denote an Englishman, often being adopted for Irish Aingléis ‘Englishman’ or through mistranslation for Mac an Ghallóglaigh, see Gallogly and Golightly .

variants.name: Engles
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Michel, Remi.
English and Scottish: variant of   English   or   Inglis  .
Altered form of German and Dutch   Engels  .

variants.name: Inglis
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire and Midlothian): ethnic name from Scots Inglis ‘English(man)’. Compare English .

variants.name: England
meanings: English: habitational name from Middle English Engelond ‘England’. It was probably a formal alternative to English , which is also well evidenced as a surname in England. These names may have been acquired by English landowners who moved in Norman social circles or who lived in a neighbouring country (Scotland, Wales, or Ireland), or by English merchants who traded abroad.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farmsteads so named, from Old Norse eng ‘meadow’ + land ‘land’.
Swedish: ornamental name with the same meaning as 2.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When George English Dalton was born in 1535, in Ipswich, Suffolk, England, his father, William Dalton, was 22 and his mother, Jane Towneley, was 20. He married Ruth Parkhurst in 1550, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 24 February 1614, in Dennington, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 79, and was buried in Dennington, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque George English Dalton est né en 1535, à Ipswich, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, William Dalton, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Jane Towneley, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Ruth Parkhurst en 1550, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 24 février 1614, à Dennington, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 79 ans, et a été enterré à Dennington, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als George English Dalton 1535 in Ipswich, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Dalton 22 und seine Mutter Jane Towneley 20. Er hat 1550 in England, United Kingdom Ruth Parkhurst geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 24. Februar 1614 im Alter von 79 Jahren in Dennington, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Dennington, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando George English Dalton è nato il nel 1535 nel Ipswich, Suffolk, England, il suo padre, William Dalton, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Jane Towneley, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Ruth Parkhurst nel 1550, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 24 febbraio 1614, nel Dennington, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 79 anni, e fu sepolto a Dennington, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: George English Daltonが1535年にイギリススフォルクイプスウィチで生まれた時，父William Daltonは22歳，母Jane Towneleyは20歳であった。1550年にイギリスイギリスでRuth Parkhurstと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1614年2月24日にイギリスイギリススフォルクデニントンで79歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススフォルクデニントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: George English Dalton(이)가 1535년 영국 수폴크 이프스위치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Dalton(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Jane Towneley(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1550년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Ruth Parkhurst(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1614년 2월 24일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 데닝턴에서 79세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 데닝턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando George English Dalton nasceu em 1535, em Ipswich, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Dalton, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Jane Towneley, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Ruth Parkhurst em 1550, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 24 de fevereiro de 1614, em Dennington, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 79 anos, e foi sepultado em Dennington, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда George English Dalton родился в 1535 г. в местности ипсвич, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени William Dalton было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Jane Towneley, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Ruth Parkhurst в 1550 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер 24 февраля 1614 г. в местности деннингтон, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 79 лет и был похоронен в местности деннингтон, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando George English Dalton nació en 1535, en Ipswich, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, William Dalton, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Jane Towneley, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Ruth Parkhurst en 1550, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 24 de febrero de 1614, en Dennington, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 79 años, y fue sepultado en Dennington, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
